commands.review_summary:
This branch adds install telemetry dedupe, batch-safe clear/prune cleanup, hard-delete dedupe cleanup, install-based search popularity, and matching docs/spec/tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This is a feature/data-model and ranking-semantics PR rather than a bug report; source inspection, tests, CI, and the inspected screenshots establish the proposed behavior path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to make the compatibility/product call and confirm Convex runtime validation; there is no narrow mechanical repair to queue from this review.

target.title: Rank skills by installs and harden install telemetry
target.body: ## Summary This PR keeps the install telemetry and search-ranking work that is still unique after #2586, and removes the stale browse recommendation-index work that #2586 already replaced. - Adds install telemetry dedupe by user, skill, root, and UTC day. - Makes telemetry clearing batch-safe and preserves fresh reinstall telemetry during an active clear. - Adds daily pruning for old install telemetry dedupe rows and cleans dedupe rows when a skill is hard-deleted. - Changes skill search popularity to use stars plus installs instead of downloads. - Updates API docs and the install telemetry/ranking spec to separate search popularity from browse recommendation score. ## What happened after #2586 ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Original #2581 branch] --> B[#2586 merged into main] B --> C[Weighted browse recommendation score now belongs to main] B --> D[Install telemetry dedupe and search popularity still unique] C --> E[Drop obsolete browse recommendation index changes] D --> F[Keep telemetry dedupe, clear safety, prune cleanup, search installs signal] E --> G[Cleaned #2581 diff] F --> G ``` ## Install telemetry path ```mermaid sequenceDiagram actor User participant Install as Install mutation participant Dedupe as installTelemetryDedupes participant Stats as install stats/search digest User->>Install: install skill from a local root Install->>Dedupe: check user + skill + root + UTC day alt first event for that day/root Install->>Dedupe: record dedupe row Install->>Stats: count install telemetry once else duplicate event Install-->>Stats: skip duplicate telemetry increment end ``` ## Clear and prune safety ```mermaid flowchart LR A[User requests telemetry clear] --> B[Create clear state with start time] B --> C[Delete telemetry rows in bounded batches] C --> D{More rows?} D -->|yes| E[Schedule next batch] E --> C D -->|no| F[Finish clear state] B --> G[Fresh reinstall after clear start] G --> H[Preserve fresh reinstall telemetry] I[Daily cron] --> J[Prune old dedupe rows] ``` ## Screenshots Prod-backed local UI proof was captured from this branch against the production Convex read backend. **Skills browse sorted by installs** **Search results for swarm** **Skill detail for chair4ce/swarm** Full proof artifacts: https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/qa-artifacts/clawhub-ui-proof/pr-2581/pr-2581-prod-data-2026-06-15T00-56-51-869Z Note: default `/plugins` was not used for proof because this branch can request `sort=recommended`, which needs matching backend code deployed with the recommendation-score API changes. The screenshots above stay on the PR's skill search/browse surface. ## Removed after #2586 - Obsolete `recommended` browse index rename to install-rank indexes. - Stale install-rank cursor/ranking tests now covered by #2586's recommendation-score path. - Unrelated CLI compatibility and publisher-abuse policy text from the new spec. ## Verification - `npm_config_registry=https://registry.npmjs.org BUN_CONFIG_REGISTRY=https://registry.npmjs.org bunx convex codegen` - `bunx vitest run convex/search.test.ts convex/telemetry.test.ts convex/crons.test.ts convex/skillStatEvents.test.ts convex/skills.exportList.test.ts convex/skills.banBatches.test.ts` - 6 files passed, 82 tests passed - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - Prod-backed local UI proof captured and published in the screenshots above.
target.author: jesse-merhi

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
